guideline: Deleted code as of 2021; use 99202-99205 for new patient visits

description: Office/outpatient visit, new patient, low complexity, 15-29 minutes
guideline: Code selection based on time or medical decision making (MDM)

description: Office/outpatient visit, new patient, moderate complexity, 30-44 minutes
guideline: Documentation must support time spent or MDM level

description: Office/outpatient visit, new patient, moderate-high complexity, 45-59 minutes
guideline: Use additional code for prolonged services if applicable (99417)

description: Office/outpatient visit, new patient, high complexity, 60-74 minutes
guideline: Requires comprehensive history and exam or high MDM

description: Office/outpatient visit, established patient, minimal presenting problem
guideline: May not require physician presence; often used for nurse visits

description: Office/outpatient visit, established patient, straightforward, 10-19 minutes
guideline: Code selection based on time or medical decision making (MDM)

description: Office/outpatient visit, established patient, low complexity, 20-29 minutes
guideline: Most commonly billed E/M code; ensure documentation supports level

description: Office/outpatient visit, established patient, moderate complexity, 30-39 minutes
guideline: Use additional code for prolonged services if exceeding time threshold

description: Office/outpatient visit, established patient, high complexity, 40-54 minutes
guideline: Requires high MDM or extensive time; document medical necessity

description: Initial hospital care, per day, low complexity
guideline: Use for first inpatient encounter; do not combine with observation codes same day

description: Initial hospital care, per day, moderate complexity
guideline: Documentation must reflect comprehensive history and exam

description: Initial hospital care, per day, high complexity
guideline: Reserved for complex admissions requiring extensive workup

description: Subsequent hospital care, per day, low complexity
guideline: Use for stable or improving patients during hospital stay

description: Subsequent hospital care, per day, moderate complexity
guideline: Requires evidence of changing clinical status

description: Subsequent hospital care, per day, high complexity
guideline: Use for unstable patients or significant new problems

description: Hospital discharge day management, 30 minutes or less
guideline: Document total time spent for discharge planning

description: Hospital discharge day management, more than 30 minutes
guideline: Use additional code for care plan oversight if applicable

description: Emergency department visit, self-limited or minor problem
guideline: Use for minimal complexity ED encounters

description: Emergency department visit, low to moderate severity
guideline: Do not use with critical care codes same encounter
Documentation must justify expanded workup

description: Emergency department visit, high severity, urgent evaluation
guideline: Use for patients requiring immediate attention but stable

description: Emergency department visit, high severity, threat to life or function
guideline: Highest level ED code; document severity and interventions
Time-based code; document total critical care time provided
Use in conjunction with 99291; report add-on units as needed

description: Initial nursing facility care, low complexity
guideline: Use for new admission to skilled nursing facility

description: Initial nursing facility care, moderate complexity
guideline: Documentation should reflect comprehensive assessment

description: Initial nursing facility care, high complexity
guideline: Reserved for complex or unstable nursing facility admissions
Use for stable, minor changes in nursing facility patients

description: Subsequent nursing facility care, low complexity
guideline: Document any changes in clinical status since last visit

description: Subsequent nursing facility care, moderate complexity
guideline: Use for patients with new or worsening problems

description: Subsequent nursing facility care, high complexity
guideline: Requires significant change in condition or high MDM

description: Home visit, new patient, low complexity
guideline: Use for new patient encounters in home setting

description: Home visit, new patient, moderate complexity
guideline: Document travel time separately if applicable

description: Home visit, established patient, low complexity
guideline: Document medical necessity for home visit vs office visit
